summary: The formal_conjectures dataset contains 4557 problems with 2,571 formalized Lean 4 conjectures and 1,029 open (sorry-marked) problems. Activity data is sparse and largely uniform: quarterly buckets q1-q10 each show exactly 100 issues, and the only per-problem detail (fc0000) shows a single resolved item. This uniformity suggests aggregate-level tracking rather than granular per-problem traction, limiting confidence in identifying specific high-activity problems.
problems: fc0000: 1 resolved issue (Proof: getCategoryStatsMarkdown), 0 currently open — appears complete
Quarterly buckets q1 through q10 each report exactly 100 issues, an implausibly uniform distribution suggesting capped/truncated reporting or placeholder data
1,029 open problems marked 'sorry' lack individual issue-level breakdowns, obscuring which open conjectures have real traction
No per-problem activity metrics available beyond fc0000, preventing reliable ranking of most-active problems
highlights: fc0000 (getCategoryStatsMarkdown proof) is fully resolved with zero open issues — a clean completed example
Strong formalization base: 2,571 conjectures already expressed in Lean 4 across number theory, combinatorics, analysis, and algebra
Clear open-problem inventory (1,029 sorry-marked) provides a well-defined backlog for targeted effort
Consistent 100-issue quarterly volume indicates sustained, ongoing engagement with the dataset

suggested_todos.title: Audit the q1-q10 buckets to confirm whether the uniform 100-issue counts are real or a reporting cap/truncation
Generate per-problem issue breakdowns for the 1,029 open (sorry) conjectures to surface genuine high-traction targets
Use fc0000's resolved workflow as a template to identify near-complete sorry-proofs that are likely solvable soon
Prioritize open problems in domains with most existing formalization (number theory, combinatorics) for next-week proof attempts
Establish a tagging convention to distinguish open, in-progress, and resolved conjectures for clearer traction tracking
